preparation_method: Mix 1.8g Sandun powder/paste with Rosa water + Komarika gel. Blend to smooth consistency.
application_instructions: 1. Cleanse face with mild cleanser and pat dry. 2. Apply thin layer of Sandun preparation on affected acne areas. 3. Avoid eye area. 4. Leave on for 11 minutes. 5. Rinse with lukewarm water. 6. Pat dry gently. 7. Apply light moisturizer if needed (especially for dry skin).
storage_instructions: Store in clean, dry container. Refrigerate if using fresh ingredients. Use within 24-48 hours for fresh preparations. Discard if odor or appearance changes.
contraindications: Safe, ensure genuine sandalwood powder. Do not use if allergic to Sandalwood or Santalum album.
possible_side_effects: Mild tingling (normal), redness (discontinue if severe), dryness (reduce frequency),
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, persistent redness >2 hours, swelling, rash, difficulty breathing, signs of allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help if: mild acne worsens after 2 weeks of treatment, severe pain, fever, signs of infection (pus, warmth, spreading redness), allergic reaction, no improvement after 2-3 weeks.
precautions_by_age: CHILDREN: Use only under adult supervision. Patch test mandatory. Use half strength. Avoid eye and mouth areas.
traditional_reference: Chandana mentioned in classical Ayurvedic texts for skin disorders
safety_category: Safe, ensure genuine sandalwood powder
patch_test_required: Yes - apply small amount on inner forearm, wait 24 hours, check for reaction
notes: Formulated for oily sensitive skin. Gentler formulation for sensitive skin. Age-appropriate dilution applied.

storage_instructions: Store in clean, dry container. Refrigerate if using fresh ingredients. Use within 24-48 hours for fresh preparations. Discard if odor or appearance changes.
contraindications: Safe, ensure genuine sandalwood powder. Do not use if allergic to Sandalwood or Santalum album.
possible_side_effects: Mild tingling (normal), redness (discontinue if severe), dryness (reduce frequency),
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, persistent redness >2 hours, swelling, rash, difficulty breathing, signs of allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help if: moderate acne worsens after 2 weeks of treatment, severe pain, fever, signs of infection (pus, warmth, spreading redness), allergic reaction, no improvement after 3-4 weeks.
precautions_by_age: CHILDREN: Use only under adult supervision. Patch test mandatory. Use half strength. Avoid eye and mouth areas.
traditional_reference: Chandana mentioned in classical Ayurvedic texts for skin disorders
safety_category: Safe, ensure genuine sandalwood powder
patch_test_required: Yes - apply small amount on inner forearm, wait 24 hours, check for reaction
notes: Formulated for normal sensitive skin. Gentler formulation for sensitive skin. Age-appropriate dilution applied.

storage_instructions: Store in clean, dry container. Refrigerate if using fresh ingredients. Use within 24-48 hours for fresh preparations. Discard if odor or appearance changes.
contraindications: Safe for topical use, may stain skin temporarily. Do not use if allergic to Turmeric or Curcuma longa. May cause photosensitivity - use sunscreen.
possible_side_effects: Mild tingling (normal), redness (discontinue if severe), dryness (reduce frequency), temporary yellow staining of skin
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, persistent redness >2 hours, swelling, rash, difficulty breathing, signs of allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help if: moderate acne worsens after 2 weeks of treatment, severe pain, fever, signs of infection (pus, warmth, spreading redness), allergic reaction, no improvement after 3-4 weeks.
precautions_by_age: TEENS: May use standard strength. Monitor for excessive drying.
traditional_reference: Haridra mentioned in classical Ayurvedic texts for skin disorders
safety_category: Safe for topical use, may stain skin temporarily
patch_test_required: Yes - apply small amount on inner forearm, wait 24 hours, check for reaction
notes: Formulated for normal sensitive skin. Gentler formulation for sensitive skin.

Rinse with lukewarm water. 6. Pat dry gently. 7. Apply light moisturizer if needed (especially for dry skin).
storage_instructions: Store in clean, dry container. Refrigerate if using fresh ingredients. Use within 24-48 hours for fresh preparations. Discard if odor or appearance changes.
contraindications: Safe, rare allergies in ragweed-sensitive people. Do not use if allergic to Chamomile or Matricaria chamomilla.
possible_side_effects: Mild tingling (normal), redness (discontinue if severe), dryness (reduce frequency),
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, persistent redness >2 hours, swelling, rash, difficulty breathing, signs of allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help if: severe acne worsens after 2 weeks of treatment, severe pain, fever, signs of infection (pus, warmth, spreading redness), allergic reaction, no improvement after 6-8 weeks.
drug_interactions: Avoid with other topical acne medications without consulting doctor.
precautions_by_age: ELDERLY: Use gentle formulation. May need extra moisturization. Check for skin fragility.
traditional_reference: Babuna mentioned in classical Ayurvedic texts for skin disorders
safety_category: Safe, rare allergies in ragweed-sensitive people
patch_test_required: Yes - apply small amount on inner forearm, wait 24 hours, check for reaction
notes: Formulated for dry sensitive skin. Includes extra moisturization for dry skin. Gentler formulation for sensitive skin. Age-appropriate dilution applied.

herb_main_compounds: Polysaccharides, glycoproteins, vitamins C and E
base_ingredient: Komarika gel + Rosa water + light Pol oil
application_duration_minutes: Leave on - do not rinse unless instructed
preparation_method: Extract fresh Komarika gel from leaf. Mix with Komarika gel + Rosa water + light Pol oil if combining. Ensure room temperature or slightly warm (not hot).
application_instructions: 1. Gently cleanse affected area with lukewarm water only (no soap). 2. Pat dry very gently - do not rub. 3. While skin still slightly damp, apply generous amount of Komarika preparation. 4. Massage very gently in circular motions. 5. Leave on - do not rinse. 6. Reapply whenever skin feels dry. 7. For mild eczema, keep skin continuously moisturized.
storage_instructions: Store in clean, airtight container. Keep away from heat and light. Refrigerate aloe gel. Use within 1 week for fresh aloe, 3 months for oils.
contraindications: Very safe, rare allergic reactions to latex in rind. Do not use if allergic to Aloe Vera or Aloe barbadensis miller. Avoid on infected or weeping eczema (seek medical help first). Check for latex allergy if using aloe (latex in rind). Patch test mandatory for eczema-prone skin.
possible_side_effects: Mild stinging on broken skin (normal but reduce if severe), temporary increased itching (may indicate healing), rare allergic reaction.
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, worsening redness, spreading of eczema, signs of infection (yellow crust, oozing, fever), allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help urgently if: Signs of infection, widespread eczema, severe itching interfering with sleep, bleeding from scratching, eczema on face in infants, no improvement after 2-4 weeks, fever or feeling unwell.
drug_interactions: Generally safe. Inform doctor if using topical steroids or other prescribed eczema treatments.
precautions_by_age: INFANTS/CHILDREN: Very gentle application. Avoid fragrant oils. Keep nails short to prevent scratching damage. Use cotton clothing. May need wet wrap therapy - consult practitioner.
traditional_reference: Kumari used traditionally for Vicharchika (eczema) in Ayurveda
safety_category: Very safe, rare allergic reactions to latex in rind
patch_test_required: MANDATORY - especially for eczema-prone skin. Test on small area for 48 hours.
notes: ECZEMA REQUIRES INTENSIVE MOISTURIZATION. Lighter but still moisturizing for oily-prone skin. Ultra-gentle formulation for sensitive skin. Avoid triggers: harsh soaps, hot water, wool clothing, stress. Keep environment humid.

application_instructions: 1. Gently cleanse affected area with lukewarm water only (no soap). 2. Pat dry very gently - do not rub. 3. While skin still slightly damp, apply generous amount of Komarika preparation. 4. Massage very gently in circular motions. 5. Leave on - do not rinse. 6. Reapply whenever skin feels dry. 7. For mild eczema, keep skin continuously moisturized.
storage_instructions: Store in clean, airtight container. Keep away from heat and light. Refrigerate aloe gel. Use within 1 week for fresh aloe, 3 months for oils.
contraindications: Very safe, rare allergic reactions to latex in rind. Do not use if allergic to Aloe Vera or Aloe barbadensis miller. Avoid on infected or weeping eczema (seek medical help first). Check for latex allergy if using aloe (latex in rind). Patch test mandatory for eczema-prone skin.
possible_side_effects: Mild stinging on broken skin (normal but reduce if severe), temporary increased itching (may indicate healing), rare allergic reaction.
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, worsening redness, spreading of eczema, signs of infection (yellow crust, oozing, fever), allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help urgently if: Signs of infection, widespread eczema, severe itching interfering with sleep, bleeding from scratching, eczema on face in infants, no improvement after 2-4 weeks, fever or feeling unwell.
drug_interactions: Generally safe. Inform doctor if using topical steroids or other prescribed eczema treatments.
precautions_by_age: ELDERLY: Extra gentle application due to fragile skin. May need even more frequent application. Monitor for skin tearing.
traditional_reference: Kumari used traditionally for Vicharchika (eczema) in Ayurveda
safety_category: Very safe, rare allergic reactions to latex in rind
patch_test_required: MANDATORY - especially for eczema-prone skin. Test on small area for 48 hours.
notes: ECZEMA REQUIRES INTENSIVE MOISTURIZATION. Extra rich formulation for dry skin. Ultra-gentle formulation for sensitive skin. Avoid triggers: harsh soaps, hot water, wool clothing, stress. Keep environment humid.

application_duration_minutes: Leave on - do not rinse unless instructed
preparation_method: Extract fresh Komarika gel from leaf. Mix with Light Pol oil + Komarika gel if combining. Ensure room temperature or slightly warm (not hot).
application_instructions: 1. Gently cleanse affected area with lukewarm water only (no soap). 2. Pat dry very gently - do not rub. 3. For moderate eczema, keep skin continuously moisturized.

when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, worsening redness, spreading of eczema, signs of infection (yellow crust, oozing, fever), allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help urgently if: Signs of infection, widespread eczema, severe itching interfering with sleep, bleeding from scratching, eczema on face in infants, no improvement after 4-6 weeks, fever or feeling unwell.

While skin still slightly damp, apply generous amount of Pol preparation. 4. Massage very gently in circular motions. 5. Leave on - do not rinse. 6. Reapply whenever skin feels dry. 7. For moderate eczema, keep skin continuously moisturized.
storage_instructions: Store in clean, airtight container. Keep away from heat and light. Refrigerate aloe gel. Use within 1 week for fresh aloe, 3 months for oils.
contraindications: Very safe, check for coconut allergy. Do not use if allergic to Coconut or Cocos nucifera. Avoid on infected or weeping eczema (seek medical help first).
possible_side_effects: Mild stinging on broken skin (normal but reduce if severe), temporary increased itching (may indicate healing), rare allergic reaction.
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, worsening redness, spreading of eczema, signs of infection (yellow crust, oozing, fever), allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help urgently if: Signs of infection, widespread eczema, severe itching interfering with sleep, bleeding from scratching, eczema on face in infants, no improvement after 4-6 weeks, fever or feeling unwell.
drug_interactions: Generally safe. Inform doctor if using topical steroids or other prescribed eczema treatments.

While skin still slightly damp, apply generous amount of Komarika preparation. 4. Massage very gently in circular motions. 5. Leave on - do not rinse. 6. Reapply whenever skin feels dry. 7. For severe eczema, keep skin continuously moisturized.
storage_instructions: Store in clean, airtight container. Keep away from heat and light. Refrigerate aloe gel. Use within 1 week for fresh aloe, 3 months for oils.
contraindications: Very safe, rare allergic reactions to latex in rind. Do not use if allergic to Aloe Vera or Aloe barbadensis miller. Avoid on infected or weeping eczema (seek medical help first). Check for latex allergy if using aloe (latex in rind).
possible_side_effects: Mild stinging on broken skin (normal but reduce if severe), temporary increased itching (may indicate healing), rare allergic reaction.
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, worsening redness, spreading of eczema, signs of infection (yellow crust, oozing, fever), allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help urgently if: Signs of infection, widespread eczema, severe itching interfering with sleep, bleeding from scratching, eczema on face in infants, no improvement after 8-12 weeks, fever or feeling unwell.
drug_interactions: Generally safe. Inform doctor if using topical steroids or other prescribed eczema treatments.
precautions_by_age: INFANTS/CHILDREN: Very gentle application. Avoid fragrant oils. Keep nails short to prevent scratching damage. Use cotton clothing. May need wet wrap therapy - consult practitioner.
traditional_reference: Kumari used traditionally for Vicharchika (eczema) in Ayurveda
safety_category: Very safe, rare allergic reactions to latex in rind
patch_test_required: MANDATORY - especially for eczema-prone skin. Test on small area for 48 hours.
notes: ECZEMA REQUIRES INTENSIVE MOISTURIZATION. Extra rich formulation for dry skin. Avoid triggers: harsh soaps, hot water, wool clothing, stress. Keep environment humid.

For severe eczema, keep skin continuously moisturized.

when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, worsening redness, spreading of eczema, signs of infection (yellow crust, oozing, fever), allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help urgently if: Signs of infection, widespread eczema, severe itching interfering with sleep, bleeding from scratching, eczema on face in infants, no improvement after 8-12 weeks, fever or feeling unwell.

While skin still slightly damp, apply generous amount of Komarika preparation. 4. Massage very gently in circular motions. 5. Leave on - do not rinse. 6. Reapply whenever skin feels dry. 7. For severe eczema, keep skin continuously moisturized.
storage_instructions: Store in clean, airtight container. Keep away from heat and light. Refrigerate aloe gel. Use within 1 week for fresh aloe, 3 months for oils.
contraindications: Very safe, rare allergic reactions to latex in rind. Do not use if allergic to Aloe Vera or Aloe barbadensis miller. Avoid on infected or weeping eczema (seek medical help first). Check for latex allergy if using aloe (latex in rind).
possible_side_effects: Mild stinging on broken skin (normal but reduce if severe), temporary increased itching (may indicate healing), rare allergic reaction.
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: severe burning, worsening redness, spreading of eczema, signs of infection (yellow crust, oozing, fever), allergic reaction.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help urgently if: Signs of infection, widespread eczema, severe itching interfering with sleep, bleeding from scratching, eczema on face in infants, no improvement after 8-12 weeks, fever or feeling unwell.
drug_interactions: Generally safe. Inform doctor if using topical steroids or other prescribed eczema treatments.
precautions_by_age: ELDERLY: Extra gentle application due to fragile skin. May need even more frequent application. Monitor for skin tearing.
traditional_reference: Kumari used traditionally for Vicharchika (eczema) in Ayurveda
safety_category: Very safe, rare allergic reactions to latex in rind
patch_test_required: MANDATORY - especially for eczema-prone skin. Test on small area for 48 hours.
notes: ECZEMA REQUIRES INTENSIVE MOISTURIZATION. Extra rich formulation for dry skin. Avoid triggers: harsh soaps, hot water, wool clothing, stress. Keep environment humid.

application_duration_minutes: 20 minutes, then can leave on or rinse
treatment_duration: 2-4 weeks - Continue 1 week after visible clearing
preparation_method: Dilute tea tree oil to 1-2% dilution in coconut oil (approximately 1-2 drops per 10ml). Ensure proper dilution.
application_instructions: 1. Wash affected area with mild soap and water, pat dry completely. 2. Apply Tea Tree preparation directly on ALL ringworm patches. 3. IMPORTANT: Apply 2-3cm BEYOND visible ring edges (fungus spreads). 4. Leave for 20 minutes. 5. Can leave on or rinse gently. 6. Wash hands thoroughly after application. 7. Continue treatment for 1 week after clearing to prevent recurrence.
storage_instructions: Store in clean, dark container away from heat. Tea tree oil degrades in light. Use within 2 weeks for fresh preparations, 6 months for diluted oils.
contraindications: Must dilute, never use undiluted, avoid ingestion. Do not use if allergic to Tea Tree Oil. NEVER use undiluted tea tree oil. Avoid in children under 2 years. Avoid in pregnancy if using tea tree oil extensively.
possible_side_effects: Mild burning/tingling (normal for antifungals), temporary redness, dryness of treated area. Strong burning indicates too concentrated - dilute more.
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: Severe burning (>5 minutes), blistering, spreading rash, severe allergic reaction. Dilute further or switch herbs.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help if: Ringworm spreads despite treatment, affects scalp or nails (needs oral medication), signs of secondary infection, no improvement after 2-4 weeks, ringworm on face (especially in children), extensive involvement, athlete has ringworm (may need systemic treatment), immunocompromised patient.
drug_interactions: Generally safe topically. Inform doctor if using other antifungal creams.
precautions_by_age: CHILDREN: Use lower concentrations. Supervise application. Prevent scratching. Treat all family members if needed. Check pets for ringworm.
traditional_reference: Tea Tree Taila used for Dadru (ringworm) in Ayurvedic texts
safety_category: Must dilute, never use undiluted, avoid ingestion
patch_test_required: Yes - especially important for strong antifungals like tea tree and garlic
notes: RINGWORM IS CONTAGIOUS. Hygiene critical: Wash hands after touching, don't share towels/clothing, treat all affected family members simultaneously, disinfect surfaces. Extra dilution for sensitive skin. Continue treatment 1 week after clearing to prevent recurrence. May need oral antifungals for severe/widespread cases.

treatment_duration: 4-6 weeks - Continue 1 week after visible clearing
preparation_method: Mix Kohomba powder/crushed with Light Pol oil or Rosa water to form paste. Ensure proper dilution.
application_instructions: 1. Wash affected area with mild soap and water, pat dry completely. 2. Apply Kohomba preparation directly on ALL ringworm patches. 3. IMPORTANT: Apply 2-3cm BEYOND visible ring edges (fungus spreads). 4. Leave for 30 minutes. 5. Can leave on or rinse gently. 6. Wash hands thoroughly after application. 7. Continue treatment for 1 week after clearing to prevent recurrence.
storage_instructions: Store in clean, dark container away from heat. Tea tree oil degrades in light. Use within 2 weeks for fresh preparations, 6 months for diluted oils.
contraindications: Generally safe, avoid in pregnancy/lactation. Do not use if allergic to Neem.
possible_side_effects: Mild burning/tingling (normal for antifungals), temporary redness, dryness of treated area.
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: Severe burning (>5 minutes), blistering, spreading rash, severe allergic reaction. Dilute further or switch herbs.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help if: Ringworm spreads despite treatment, affects scalp or nails (needs oral medication), signs of secondary infection, no improvement after 4-6 weeks, ringworm on face (especially in children), extensive involvement, athlete has ringworm (may need systemic treatment), immunocompromised patient.
drug_interactions: Generally safe topically. Inform doctor if using other antifungal creams.
precautions_by_age: CHILDREN: Use lower concentrations. Supervise application. Prevent scratching. Treat all family members if needed. Check pets for ringworm.
traditional_reference: Nimba used for Dadru (ringworm) in Ayurvedic texts
safety_category: Generally safe, avoid in pregnancy/lactation
patch_test_required: Yes - especially important for strong antifungals like tea tree and garlic
notes: RINGWORM IS CONTAGIOUS. Hygiene critical: Wash hands after touching, don't share towels/clothing, treat all affected family members simultaneously, disinfect surfaces. Continue treatment 1 week after clearing to prevent recurrence. May need oral antifungals for severe/widespread cases.

notes: RINGWORM IS CONTAGIOUS. Hygiene critical: Wash hands after touching, don't share towels/clothing, treat all affected family members simultaneously, disinfect surfaces. Continue treatment 1 week after clearing to prevent recurrence. May need oral antifungals for severe/widespread cases.

application_duration_minutes: 30 minutes, then can leave on or rinse
treatment_duration: 4-6 weeks - Continue 1 week after visible clearing
preparation_method: Dilute tea tree oil to 3-5% dilution in coconut oil (approximately 2-3 drops per 10ml). Ensure proper dilution.
application_instructions: 1. Wash affected area with mild soap and water, pat dry completely. 2. Apply Tea Tree preparation directly on ALL ringworm patches. 3. IMPORTANT: Apply 2-3cm BEYOND visible ring edges (fungus spreads). 4. Leave for 30 minutes. 5. Can leave on or rinse gently. 6. Wash hands thoroughly after application. 7. Continue treatment for 1 week after clearing to prevent recurrence.
storage_instructions: Store in clean, dark container away from heat. Tea tree oil degrades in light. Use within 2 weeks for fresh preparations, 6 months for diluted oils.
contraindications: Must dilute, never use undiluted, avoid ingestion. Do not use if allergic to Tea Tree Oil. NEVER use undiluted tea tree oil. Avoid in children under 2 years. Avoid in pregnancy if using tea tree oil extensively.
possible_side_effects: Mild burning/tingling (normal for antifungals), temporary redness, dryness of treated area. Strong burning indicates too concentrated - dilute more.
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: Severe burning (>5 minutes), blistering, spreading rash, severe allergic reaction. Dilute further or switch herbs.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help if: Ringworm spreads despite treatment, affects scalp or nails (needs oral medication), signs of secondary infection, no improvement after 4-6 weeks, ringworm on face (especially in children), extensive involvement, athlete has ringworm (may need systemic treatment), immunocompromised patient.
drug_interactions: Generally safe topically. Inform doctor if using other antifungal creams.
precautions_by_age: ATHLETES: Treat shoes and equipment. Use antifungal powder in shoes. Dry thoroughly after sports.

Continue treatment for 1 week after clearing to prevent recurrence.
storage_instructions: Store in clean, dark container away from heat. Tea tree oil degrades in light. Use within 2 weeks for fresh preparations, 6 months for diluted oils.
contraindications: Must dilute, never use undiluted, avoid ingestion. Do not use if allergic to Tea Tree Oil. NEVER use undiluted tea tree oil. Avoid in children under 2 years. Avoid in pregnancy if using tea tree oil extensively.
possible_side_effects: Mild burning/tingling (normal for antifungals), temporary redness, dryness of treated area. Strong burning indicates too concentrated - dilute more.
when_to_stop: Stop immediately if: Severe burning (>5 minutes), blistering, spreading rash, severe allergic reaction. Dilute further or switch herbs.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical help if: Ringworm spreads despite treatment, affects scalp or nails (needs oral medication), signs of secondary infection, no improvement after 6-8 weeks, ringworm on face (especially in children), extensive involvement, athlete has ringworm (may need systemic treatment), immunocompromised patient.
drug_interactions: Generally safe topically. Inform doctor if using other antifungal creams.
precautions_by_age: ATHLETES: Treat shoes and equipment. Use antifungal powder in shoes. Dry thoroughly after sports.
traditional_reference: Tea Tree Taila used for Dadru (ringworm) in Ayurvedic texts
safety_category: Must dilute, never use undiluted, avoid ingestion
patch_test_required: Yes - especially important for strong antifungals like tea tree and garlic
notes: RINGWORM IS CONTAGIOUS. Hygiene critical: Wash hands after touching, don't share towels/clothing, treat all affected family members simultaneously, disinfect surfaces. Continue treatment 1 week after clearing to prevent recurrence. May need oral antifungals for severe/widespread cases.
